note: AUD5 fix for finding F-02 (docs/novakai/audit/04-findings.md). SCOPE: .mjs TOOLING + one CI workflow edit — real verification is node --test tools/novakai/handoff-fresh.test.mjs (3 fail-closed tests red pre-fix, 10/10 post-fix).

intent.problem: F4's CI enforcement was structurally vacuous: spec-gate checked out at depth 1, so every `git log -1 -- <path>` resolved to the boundary commit — a permanent same-commit tie (proven live on PR #1). Plus AUD2 A3: dirty-handoff bypass, and a git-error catch that vacuously passed; AUD3 T1/M1: the --check deny path had zero tests.
intent.approach: --check fails CLOSED on shallow clones (git rev-parse --is-shallow-repository) and on git errors; the dirty-handoff bypass becomes LOCAL-only (ignored under CI); spec-gate's novakai-drift job checks out with fetch-depth: 0 (Chris's mandate). Five CLI deny tests in deterministic fixture repos (GIT_COMMITTER_DATE-pinned): stale->1, fresh->0, shallow->1, no-repo->1, CI-dirty->recomputed — the shallow/no-repo/CI-dirty three were red pre-fix.
intent.rationale: what the gate cannot prove it must not pass; the accepted boundary (timestamp freshness, same-commit tie passes for atomic code+handoff commits, H5 content check counters empty touches) is now stated in the tool header instead of overstated.
